title: Protect System Media Containing CUI Both Paper and Digital
description: protect (i.e., physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI, both paper and digital

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of paper media containing CUI including locked storage, access restrictions, and physical inventory is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of digital media containing CUI including locked storage, cable locks, and physical access restrictions is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of paper media containing CUI in locked cabinets or safes in controlled areas is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of digital media containing CUI including encrypted storage, locked containers, and access-controlled environments is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of paper media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of digital media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of paper media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of digital media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of paper media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of digital media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of paper media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of digital media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of paper media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Physically Controlled
checks.purpose: Physical control of digital media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Paper Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of paper media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Digital Media Containing CUI is Securely Stored
checks.purpose: Secure storage of digital media is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

remediation: Establish and document a media protection policy covering physical control and secure storage of both paper and digital media containing CUI. Implement locked storage containers, access-controlled rooms, and media inventories. Ensure digital media is encrypted at rest and stored in physically secured locations accessible only to authorized personnel.
